item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like a start of a very good scip with tons of interesting exploration…
Writing is good, idea solid. But when I got hooked, I received a hint of what is in 3 out of 12 floors of the anomaly.

Floor 11-> Computing devices… Foundation would definitely try to crack into the file storage to find out what is/was going on. Possibly thing if they can use the computing power.

Floor 6-> Habitating area of what definitely does not look like humans. DNA tests, Chemical tests, All sorts of test regarding the material, residual materials, food, and technology used to prepare the food. Again nice hook. But there is nothing behind it.

Floor 1-> A lot of door opening, some medical tools and human skeletons. Someone or something definitely studied the humans. + some green unopenable doors which feels like connection to the owner of the place.

For me this is downvote. Because there is so much hooks that the article just ends abruptly, leaving reader disappointed after a lot of promises. I am curious about the anomaly, I can see its potential, but its not there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the clinical tone was touched up I'd probably like it. But as it stands, I'll have to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I miss the good old days. Always preferred the style of back then, if not the writing quality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept of it seems interesting enough, but there doesn't seem to be much of a hook here. It could certainly be fleshed out a little more.

A minor nitpick: "Sievert" is a unit of radiation dosage, not radioactivity. You'd be looking for Bequerels (Bq, decays/second) or Grays (Energy deposited per kg.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And one more. A habitat for aliens and an arbitrary time lock for people entering it. Weird, but not interesting. Having said that, I understand why such a document would exist in-universe. For me, though, that is not a good enough reason to write it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirding. There's what feels like the start of a mystery but it never got intriguing enough to really draw me in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels like the concept behind the 3000 contest was a harbinger for a renaissance in series I style.

It would seem to be a bit of a rebirth for the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More or less this? There's a few clues thrown in, but a lot of things like the specificity of the time spans seem to serve no purpose. And overall, it's just a spatial anomaly containing the remnants of something that's over and done with. I guess?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Darn, a lot of short SCPs have been posted recently.

On one hand, this is pretty well written, and I can tell that there's something more going on in here. On the other hand, it's not particularly compelling to me, and I wasn't able to be invested in the SCP proper.

Novote.

item.title: THREE BEDROOMS IN A GOOD NEIGHBORHOOD ON FIRE LIKE A MARGARITA MADE OUT OF WOOD
item.content:encoded:
She got solar panels for thirty cents a watt
In the future, there’s a party from the past, she’s a cop

8
